title: Gynochthodes scortechinii
intro: Gynochthodes scortechinii, also known as the Malaysian forest vine, is a type of plant species in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Gynochthodes scortechinii include iridoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gynochthodes scortechinii is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusions, and may also be used to make tinctures or capsules for consumption.
taxonomy: Gynochthodes scortechinii is classified within the family Clusiaceae, commonly known as the St. John's Wort family. This family consists of flowering plants native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. Gynochthodes scortechinii, specifically, is a species within the genus Gynochthodes, which is comprised of flowering plants found in tropical Asia. The classification of Gynochthodes scortechinii reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its native environment. The detailed classification of Gynochthodes scortechinii is essential for understanding its evolutionary relationships and ecological role within its ecosystem.

Its leaves and stems are used to make infusions and decoctions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
